# dedalus_workspace (Resource)



## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "dedalus_workspace" "example_workspace" {
  memory_mib = 0
  storage_gib = 0
  vcpu = 0
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `memory_mib` (Number) Memory in MiB.
- `storage_gib` (Number)
- `vcpu` (Number) CPU in vCPUs.

### Optional

- `timeouts` (Attributes) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--timeouts))

### Read-Only

- `desired_state` (String) Available values: "active", "inactive", "destroyed".
- `id` (String) The ID of this resource.
- `schema` (String) A URL to the JSON Schema for this object.
- `status` (Attributes) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--status))
- `workspace_id` (String)

<a id="nestedatt--timeouts"></a>
### Nested Schema for `timeouts`

Optional:

- `create` (String) The timeout for the operation, default: 600 seconds
- `delete` (String) The timeout for the operation, default: 600 seconds
- `read` (String) The timeout for the operation, default: 120 seconds
- `update` (String) The timeout for the operation, default: 600 seconds


<a id="nestedatt--status"></a>
### Nested Schema for `status`

Read-Only:

- `last_error` (String)
- `last_progress_at` (String)
- `last_transition_at` (String)
- `phase` (String) Available values: "accepted", "placement_pending", "starting", "running", "stopping", "sleeping", "destroying", "destroyed", "failed".
- `reason` (String)
- `retryable` (Boolean)
- `revision` (String)

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

The [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import) can be used, for example:

```shell
$ terraform import dedalus_workspace.example '<workspace_id>'
```
